architecture_fit.note: Solves the task perfectly but via a fixed conv2d that re-encodes the exact GoL rule — no attention, a large departure from base_model.py.

baseline_comparison.note: Harness static-copy baseline (F1~0.52) is beaten convincingly (lift 0.48 canonical), but the attempt adds no own no-attention/no-circuit strawman.

faithfulness.note: Hand-built circuit with no trained model and no ablation; README does not discuss faithfulness or propose a causal check.

operating_range.note: Perfect F1 across the full density sweep (0.1-0.5), but only ~half an order of magnitude and no scale/grid-size stress test or breakage shown.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely hand-set circuit (kernel + scale/offset) reproducing the target rule exactly — exactly what this bonus rewards.

visual_judgement.note: Text-board demo where pred and truth call the SAME _gol_step, so the match panel is trivially 100% — it compares the circuit to itself, not an independent ground truth.

visualisation_rationale.note: README describes a kernel heatmap, scale slider and accuracy heatmap that do not exist in app.py (which renders monospace boards) — rationale does not match the implementation.

notes: A hand-built fixed-convolution circuit that computes the exact toroidal Game of Life update, scoring a perfect 1.0 on every automated metric and earning full marks on the hardcoded-weights bonus. But it sidesteps the goal's real question: it uses no attention and is a large rewrite away from base_model.py, with no trained model or faithfulness/ablation evidence. The demo is also weak — it compares the circuit against itself (pred and truth share one code path), and the README describes heatmaps that the actual app does not render.
